description: Operational review checks for cloud-hosted services. Covers service health, deployment, on-call readiness, data management, and capacity planning.

checkpoints.title: SLOs defined with measurable targets
checkpoints.description: Service Level Objectives must be defined and documented with measurable targets.
checkpoints.fix_hint: Create a docs/SLO.md defining availability, latency, and error rate targets with measurement methods.

checkpoints.title: Service metadata file exists
checkpoints.description: A service metadata file declares ownership, tier, dependencies, and classification.
checkpoints.fix_hint: Add a service.yaml at the repo root declaring: name, owner, tier, on-call contact, and dependencies.

checkpoints.title: Rollback procedures documented
checkpoints.description: Step-by-step rollback procedures must be documented and tested before production launch.
checkpoints.fix_hint: Create a docs/ROLLBACK.md with step-by-step instructions for rolling back each type of deployment.

checkpoints.title: Deployment regions documented
checkpoints.description: Deployment topology — regions, availability zones, and multi-region strategy — must be documented.
checkpoints.fix_hint: Document deployment regions in your README or a dedicated deployment topology doc.

checkpoints.title: Progressive deployment or feature flags configured
checkpoints.description: Deployments should use progressive rollout (canary, ring-based, or feature flags) to limit blast radius.
checkpoints.fix_hint: Implement feature flags or canary deployments to safely roll out changes to a subset of traffic.

checkpoints.title: On-call rotation documented
checkpoints.description: DRI (Directly Responsible Individual) rotation and escalation path must be documented.
checkpoints.fix_hint: Create a docs/ONCALL.md with the current on-call rotation, escalation path, and bridge/channel details.

checkpoints.title: Post-mortem template exists
checkpoints.description: A post-mortem template must be available for incident retrospectives.
checkpoints.fix_hint: Add a post-mortem template to docs/. Include: timeline, impact, root cause, action items.

checkpoints.title: Support channel documented
checkpoints.description: The team's support channel (Slack, Teams, email alias) must be documented and discoverable.
checkpoints.fix_hint: Add your team's support channel link to the README or a SUPPORT.md file.

checkpoints.title: Safe production access interfaces documented
checkpoints.description: How engineers safely access and modify production (safe touch interfaces) must be documented.
checkpoints.fix_hint: Create runbooks describing how to safely interact with production: approved query patterns, config changes, and emergency procedures.

checkpoints.title: Data retention policies documented
checkpoints.description: Data retention and deletion policies must be documented for all data stores.
checkpoints.fix_hint: Document data retention periods and deletion procedures for each data store in docs/DATA_RETENTION.md.

checkpoints.title: Failure modes documented
checkpoints.description: Known failure modes and degraded-state behaviors must be documented.
checkpoints.fix_hint: Document how the service behaves when dependencies fail. Include: what degrades gracefully, what fails hard, and recovery paths.

checkpoints.title: RTO/RPO documented for critical data stores
checkpoints.description: Recovery Time and Recovery Point Objectives must be defined for all critical data stores.
checkpoints.fix_hint: Add RTO and RPO targets to your SLO doc or a dedicated disaster recovery doc.

checkpoints.title: Capacity planning documented
checkpoints.description: Expected load, scaling limits, and capacity headroom must be documented.
checkpoints.fix_hint: Document peak expected traffic, scaling triggers, and headroom. Include auto-scale config or manual scaling procedures.

checkpoints.title: Testing strategy documented
checkpoints.description: The overall testing strategy — what's covered, at what layers, and what's excluded — must be documented.
checkpoints.fix_hint: Create a docs/TESTING.md describing your test pyramid: unit, integration, e2e, load, chaos.
